title: Default Credentials on the Management Console
objective: Log into the exposed management console using default credentials.
briefing: Recon shows a Tomcat manager console at http://{ip}:8080/manager/html, publicly reachable. It's unlikely anyone ever changed the factory defaults.
hints: Admin panels installed from a default package often ship with default credentials that get left in place.
Try the most obvious possible username/password pair, not a clever one.
debrief: The management console was left running with its out-of-the-box default credentials (admin:admin) - nobody changed them, and nobody disabled the console for an environment that doesn't need it exposed. This is Security Misconfiguration (OWASP A05:2021): a secure feature (authentication) existed, but was deployed with an insecure default. The fix is twofold - change or remove every default credential before going live, and disable/restrict management interfaces that don't need to be internet-facing at all.

cases.output:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Manager console access granted

logs_append: [CONFIG] manager console accessed using default credentials admin:admin
[AUTH] failed manager login attempt for user '{user}'
